title: Secrets Logged to Console or Log File
description: Logging passwords, tokens, or API keys to application logs violates SOC2 CC7.2 (monitoring) and CC6.1 (access controls). Log aggregators often store data in plaintext and are accessible to operations teams.

finding.message: Potential secret logged at '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Mask or redact sensitive fields before logging: use structured logging with field exclusions.
Implement a log sanitizer/redactor middleware that strips secrets from log records.
Use a secrets scanning tool in your CI pipeline (e.g., truffleHog, gitleaks) to catch log leaks.
Consider log access controls: limit who can query logs containing sensitive operation traces.
